behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-model diarization/label quality (LLM-DEP equivalent for STT model output), which deterministic stubs would hide (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: behavior text focuses on STT output preservation, not a required multi-agentic chain. Rejected System: network/API transport can be stubbed, but label correctness cannot.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real multi-speaker meeting audio sample with known speaker turns, call the real speech-to-text API, and assert transcript segments preserve correct speaker labels needed for attendee attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is local parsing/transformation logic on transcript text; no real OS/network/model dependency is required (MOCK-MISS says this is a single-function boundary). Rejected Integration: behavior text is specifically "parsing extracts ... into structure," which can fail within one parser function.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser function transcript lines containing explicit assignments (e.g., "Dana will draft the RFC") and assert exact assignee/task fields in produced summary-input object.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output content/coverage across attendees; stubbing summarizer would mask omission failures (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: behavior text requires one summary-output quality condition and does not require validating multiple agentic handoffs in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript fixture with explicit assignments for multiple attendees, run real summarization, and assert each assigned attendee appears with >=1 action item in output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic routing/mapping logic after decisions are already identified; failure boundary is a local function/object. Rejected Integration: behavior text says "routes identified decisions," which isolates to schema-routing logic, not module interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Call decision-routing function with pre-identified decision items and narrative text, then assert decisions land only in `decisions` schema fields and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This tests model judgment/extraction quality and placement in output sections; deterministic fixtures would always pass and miss real misses/misplacement (LLM-DEP, deterministic-assertion trap). Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets single summarization outcome from transcript, not a required multi-agentic pipeline validation.
behaviors.test_description: Run real summarization on transcripts with explicit key decisions and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` section with acceptable semantic match, and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires segmented sequential processing plus merge coherence, i.e., multiple agentic calls in sequence with orchestration/handoff (MULTI-STEP). Integration is disqualified by real model dependence and sequencing across stages; Agentic alone is too low because it misses cross-step merge/orchestration failures.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, execute production segmentation pipeline (segment -> transcribe/summarize per segment -> merge), and assert ordered processing, no dropped segments, and coherent merged structured summary.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end multi-stage execution with real transcription and summarization steps; failure can occur at handoff/orchestration across agentic stages (MULTI-STEP). System is necessary but insufficient because core correctness includes sequential agentic pipeline completion.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real meeting recording through production-like pipeline from upload to delivered structured output, asserting automatic completion, valid schema, and no manual retries/intervention.
